creator: michael.hmich@gmail.com
text:
the NTFS / Linux-Data is strange, long time ago this partition was part of a Ntfs raid for sharing data with soem windows guys, later it was deleted and a new linux partition was created on it and at first I didn't noticed it, much later I saw it but since it didn't made problems I just let it be

% e2fsck -f -n /dev/ada0p5
% dumpe2fs /dev/ada0p5

what really matters is ada0s1, nevertheless, any of the commands with any of the partitions return them as not existent or superblock not found

Martin get the toast now with this suggestion

kern.geom.label.disk_ident.enable=0

and the partition becaome visible, in /dev/ they are and gpart output follows

hm@hm-fbsd:~ % gpart show ada0
=>       63  976770992  ada0  MBR  (466G)
         63       1985        - free -  (993K)
       2048  122880860     1  linux-data  [active]  (59G)
  122882908       5282        - free -  (2.6M)
  122888190   98299906     2  ebr  (47G)
  221188096  716804096     3  linux-data  (342G)
  937992192   38774784     4  ntfs  (18G)
  976766976       4079        - free -  (2.0M)



but still I can not mount them, same errors as before

dmesg shows

GEOM_PART: integrity check failed (ada0s2, EBR
ext2fs: ada0s2: wrong magic num
ber 0 (expected 0xef53)
GEOM_PART: integrity check failed (ada0s2, EBR)

ada0s2 is an extended partitionand holding s4, s5 and a 8g linux swap partition which doesn't appear
